title: On the Limits of Reason
author: From: Notes from Underground by Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: Man is a creature that walks on two legs and is ungrateful. He is not a purely rational machine, and his greatest 'mistakes' often define his humanity.
action: Action: Acknowledge an irrationality in your decision-making and build a system to compensate for it.

title: The OODA Loop
author: From: John Boyd
content: Observe, Orient, Decide, Act. The speed at which you move through this cycle determines your competitive edge.
action: Action: Shorten the time between 'deciding' and 'acting' on your next task.

title: The Law of Unintended Consequences
author: From: Systems Thinking
content: Interventions in complex systems always have secondary effects. Always ask: 'What happens next?'
action: Action: Think one step beyond your current project—what is the second-order result?

title: The Minimum Viable Product
author: From: The Lean Startup
content: Build the simplest version that allows you to learn. Perfection is an iteration, not a starting point.
action: Action: Strip your next deliverable down to the single most critical feature.

title: The Endowment Effect
author: From: Dan Ariely
content: We value things more simply because we own them. Objectivity requires looking at your work as if you were a buyer, not a creator.
action: Action: Ask yourself, 'If I didn't create this, would I use it?' and adjust accordingly.
